item.text: I’ll inspect the working-tree diff and the surrounding code paths, then use only non-test static checks if useful. I won’t run the test suite.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,180p' lib/get-html-headers.js; sed -n '1,240p' lib/transform.js; rg -n \"getHtmlHeaders|id:|countHeaders|addAnchor\" lib test doctoc.js"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,180p' lib/get-html-headers.js; sed -n '1,240p' lib/transform.js; rg -n \"getHtmlHeaders|id:|countHeaders|addAnchor\" lib test doctoc.js"

item.text: The change is narrowly scoped to propagating HTML `id` attributes and bypassing generated-slug counting. I’m checking edge cases where raw HTML IDs interact with Markdown link syntax and with the existing heading parser.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "node -e \"var t=require('./lib/transform'); ['a)b','a(b','a b','a#b'].forEach(function(id){var c='<"'!-- START doctoc -->'"\\\\n<"'!-- END doctoc -->'"\\\\n

Title

\\\\n'; console.log(id, JSON.stringify(t(c).toc))})\" && node -e \"var md=require('@textlint/markdown-to-ast'); ['- [Title](#a)b)','- [Title](#a(b)','- [Title](#a%20b)','- [Title](#a#b)'].forEach(function(s){try{console.log(s, JSON.stringify(md.parse(s).children[0]))}catch(e){console.log('ERR',s,e.message)}})\""
